text: Hello and welcome to this episode of This is My Architecture. My name is Kapil and with me today
I have Sapna from Axiom SL. Sapna, welcome to the show. Hey, nice to be here. Glad to have you here,
Sapna. Can you introduce us to Axiom SL? Sure. So Axiom SL's controller view is essentially a risk
and reporting platform. We allow financial institutions to load native data, enrich,
aggregate it, map it to regulatory templates, all through a no-code transparent experience.
That's fantastic. And obviously Axiom SL has been around for several years. Can you tell us what
was the motivation to build a SaaS platform on AWS? It was the industry's change of view on cloud,
really. So the industry went from unacceptable on cloud to a matured stance of this is a key
business enabler. And regulators globally identified this, assessed cloud risk, and provided guidelines.
So what financial institutions realized was they can work around data sovereignty and security concerns
so long as they have the right in-house team or vendor. Axiom SL, already having a strong footprint
in the regulatory reporting industry, thought we were best placed to capture cloud benefits and
bring this to our clients. Got it. And so can you tell us what do you actually deploy on AWS on behalf
of your customers? Sure. So we essentially do an automated deployment of a single tenant isolated
application stack as you see here. And this exists per environment, per client. Wow. So that's,
that must be hundreds of different environments that you have to provision and orchestrate. How do
you do that efficiently? So automation is key to it. We try end-to-end industry standard automation. So we
achieve this using Terraform for IAC, Jenkins for continuous integration, Ansible for configuration, of
course, and Amazon's ECS. And then further, CIS-hardened images are built through AMI, again, for every
environment and every client. Got it. So every update to the application, you package it up as a CIS-hardened image and
deploy into the client environments. Obviously, security must be top of mind for Axiom SL. What other security
features do you have in your platform? So security is the center of everything we do in our application stack to our
processes. We achieve this using native AWS services. So namely, KMS Security Hub and Guard Duty give us the
standard of security we are looking for. And then we automate security response to incidents using AWS
Config, CloudWatch, CloudTrail. Not to forget, S3 is used quite heavily as well to provide end-to-end data
encryption, both for transit as well as at rest. That's fantastic. And I see there's another AWS native
service that you're using, Amazon Redshift. Can you tell us a bit about that? So Redshift came to as really a
problem solver for us because we were looking at the statement of extremely large volumes of client data in
the last few years because of increase in granular regulatory requirements. And we were looking for
technologies which could process this data quickly, come at a reasonable price, little expertise needed from us,
proven track record of continuous service availability. And all of this was checked by Redshift, especially if you
consider the recently available smaller RA3 nodes, because they allow us to scale independently for storage versus compute.
Absolutely. And speaking of scaling storage, do you also happen to use Redshift Spectrum?
Yeah, we're actually very excited about it. So Redshift Spectrum essentially allows us to give our clients a warm storage capability.
What we mean by that is regulators often ask clients for MIS on historical data, and this is on short notice more often than not,
which means that financial institutions need to do all of this with quick turnaround time so they don't have to wait for data
unarchive, a load, and then for the processing. And Redshift Spectrum helps us do that.
Got it. So with Redshift, you're able to deliver better performance, better cost efficiency for your customers.
Tell us what was the experience like for your application to integrate with Redshift?
Right. So our application is database agnostic to begin with. So it was relatively easier to kick off Redshift
and cooperation into our ecosystem. Having said that though, there were changes to be made.
For example, we have to separate OLTP data workload processing from OLAP.
Right.
We also need to obviously support Spectrum and then work around Redshift's inherent table limit design.
Right. But you were able to overcome those challenges and deliver relatively much better performance and cost efficiency.
Tell us what's next for Axiom SL or RedCloud.
Right. So Axiom SL are all in for into RedCloud, especially because firms of all types really need to embrace cloud now.
So in addition to the usual optimizations that one would expect in terms of performance, cost, scalability on any application,
we are very excited with the results that we see on AI, ML, distributed processing, higher computation.
And we are looking forward to bringing these additional value adds to our clients.
All really exciting stuff, Sapna.
Thank you so much for sharing these insights with our customers and viewers.
And thank you for watching this episode of This Is My Architecture.
